forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTBNPQQJ45YWGVSY10NR4RX9
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8f80
+project: local-review
+title: Migrate safety rules (command-safety, no-secrets, test-integrity) to review-time rules
+---
+## What
+With hooks retired, the safety/integrity rules no longer fire in real time — they become review-time rules in the security and tests dimensions. Re-home the existing `builtin/validators/security-rules/` (input-validation, no-secrets) and `builtin/validators/test-integrity/` (no-test-cheating), plus the command-safety rules, into the no-`trigger` review format under their dimensions.
+
+- Convert each to the new format (drop `trigger`, keep `match.files` globs; secrets/command rules may match broadly).
+- Map to dimensions: `security` (input-validation, no-secrets, command-safety) and `tests` (no-test-cheating, plus the existing `no-hard-code` "return 42 to pass a test" rule).
+- Because these used to BLOCK at hook time, make their review severity `blocker` so a confirmed secret or injection still stops the work via the review column gate.
+- Document clearly (in the rule bodies and the design doc) that command-safety is now an after-the-fact review finding, not a pre-execution block — this is the accepted trade-off from retiring hooks.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] security-rules, test-integrity, and command-safety rules exist in the new format, mapped to the `security`/`tests` dimensions, severity `blocker`, no `trigger`.
+- [ ] The loader resolves them and the fleet runs them as dimensions.
+- [ ] Rule bodies/design note state the real-time→review-time change explicitly.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Loader/parse test: the migrated rule sets parse and match expected paths under the security/tests dimensions.
+- [ ] Fixture review test: a temp file with a planted hardcoded secret → the security dimension produces a confirmed blocker finding through the engine (scripted agent).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` for the fixture review test; the rest is content migration. Reuse the existing rule wording; only reformat and re-home. Depends on the quality-rules task so the dimension layout already exists.
\ No newline at end of file

 title: Migrate safety rules (command-safety, no-secrets, test-integrity) to review-time rules

 ## What
-With hooks retired, the safety/integrity rules no longer fire in real time — they become review-time rules in the security and tests dimensions. Re-home the existing `builtin/validators/security-rules/` (input-validation, no-secrets) and `builtin/validators/test-integrity/` (no-test-cheating), plus the command-safety rules, into the no-`trigger` review format under their dimensions.
+With hooks retired, the safety/integrity rules no longer fire in real time — they become review-time validators. Re-home the existing `builtin/validators/security-rules/` (input-validation, no-secrets), `builtin/validators/test-integrity/` (no-test-cheating), and the command-safety rules into the no-`trigger` review format as focused validators.

-- Convert each to the new format (drop `trigger`, keep `match.files` globs; secrets/command rules may match broadly).
-- Map to dimensions: `security` (input-validation, no-secrets, command-safety) and `tests` (no-test-cheating, plus the existing `no-hard-code` "return 42 to pass a test" rule).
-- Because these used to BLOCK at hook time, make their review severity `blocker` so a confirmed secret or injection still stops the work via the review column gate.

+- Convert each to the new format (drop `trigger`, keep `match.files` globs; add `probes` where a check benefits from one; secrets/command rules may match broadly).
+- Keep them as focused validators: `security` (input-validation, no-secrets, command-safety) and `test-integrity` (no-test-cheating, plus the existing `no-hard-code` "return 42 to pass a test" rule).
+- Because these used to BLOCK at hook time, set their severity `blocker` so a confirmed secret or injection still stops the work via the review-column gate.
+- Document clearly (in the rule bodies and the design doc) that command-safety is now an after-the-fact review finding, not a pre-execution block — the accepted trade-off from retiring hooks.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] security-rules, test-integrity, and command-safety rules exist in the new format, mapped to the `security`/`tests` dimensions, severity `blocker`, no `trigger`.
-- [ ] The loader resolves them and the fleet runs them as dimensions.
+- [ ] `security` and `test-integrity` validators exist in the new format, severity `blocker`, no `trigger`.
+- [ ] The loader resolves them and the fleet runs them as their own agents.
 - [ ] Rule bodies/design note state the real-time→review-time change explicitly.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Loader/parse test: the migrated rule sets parse and match expected paths under the security/tests dimensions.
-- [ ] Fixture review test: a temp file with a planted hardcoded secret → the security dimension produces a confirmed blocker finding through the engine (scripted agent).
+- [ ] Loader/parse test: the migrated validators parse and match expected paths.
+- [ ] Fixture review test: a temp file with a planted hardcoded secret → the `security` validator produces a confirmed blocker finding through the engine (scripted agent).
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` for the fixture review test; the rest is content migration. Reuse the existing rule wording; only reformat and re-home. Depends on the quality-rules task so the dimension layout already exists.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,25 +6,30 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: 8f80
 project: local-review
-title: Migrate safety rules (command-safety, no-secrets, test-integrity) to review-time rules
+title: Migrate safety rules to focused review-time validators (no-secrets, injection, command-safety, test-integrity)
 ---
 ## What
-With hooks retired, the safety/integrity rules no longer fire in real time — they become review-time validators. Re-home the existing `builtin/validators/security-rules/` (input-validation, no-secrets), `builtin/validators/test-integrity/` (no-test-cheating), and the command-safety rules into the no-`trigger` review format as focused validators.
+With hooks retired, the safety/integrity rules no longer fire in real time — they become review-time validators. Re-home the existing `builtin/validators/security-rules/` (input-validation, no-secrets), `builtin/validators/test-integrity/` (no-test-cheating), and the command-safety rules into the no-`trigger` format as FOCUSED, one-concern validators (same split philosophy as the quality validators):

-- Convert each to the new format (drop `trigger`, keep `match.files` globs; add `probes` where a check benefits from one; secrets/command rules may match broadly).
-- Keep them as focused validators: `security` (input-validation, no-secrets, command-safety) and `test-integrity` (no-test-cheating, plus the existing `no-hard-code` "return 42 to pass a test" rule).
-- Because these used to BLOCK at hook time, set their severity `blocker` so a confirmed secret or injection still stops the work via the review-column gate.
-- Document clearly (in the rule bodies and the design doc) that command-safety is now an after-the-fact review finding, not a pre-execution block — the accepted trade-off from retiring hooks.
+| Validator | Concern | `probes` | severity |
+|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
+| `no-secrets` | secret-looking literals committed to code | (none) | blocker |
+| `injection` | unvalidated input → SQL/command/XSS/template injection | (none) | blocker |
+| `command-safety` | dangerous shell patterns in scripts/commands in the diff | (none) | blocker |
+| `test-integrity` | test cheating + the `no-hard-code` "return 42 to pass a test" rule (moved here from code-quality) | (none) | blocker |

+- All are in-file judgments → no probes.
+- **Document the real-time→review-time shift, especially for `command-safety`:** as a hook it blocked a *proposed* command (`rm -rf /`) before execution; as a review validator there is no proposed command, so it reviews **shell scripts / commands embedded in the diff** for dangerous patterns — narrower, and after-the-fact. The rule body must say this plainly so it's not mistaken for a real-time guard. Same note applies generally: confirmed secrets/injection now stop work via the review-column gate (blocker), not a pre-execution block.
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `security` and `test-integrity` validators exist in the new format, severity `blocker`, no `trigger`.
-- [ ] The loader resolves them and the fleet runs them as their own agents.
-- [ ] Rule bodies/design note state the real-time→review-time change explicitly.
+- [ ] `no-secrets`, `injection`, `command-safety`, `test-integrity` exist as separate focused validators in the new format, blocker severity, no `trigger`, no probes.
+- [ ] `no-hard-code` lives in `test-integrity`; the old `security-rules`/`test-integrity` multi-rule sets are re-homed.
+- [ ] `command-safety` (and a general note) document the real-time→review-time change.
+- [ ] `check validators` reports OK for the migrated set.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Loader/parse test: the migrated validators parse and match expected paths.
-- [ ] Fixture review test: a temp file with a planted hardcoded secret → the `security` validator produces a confirmed blocker finding through the engine (scripted agent).
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
+- [ ] Loader/parse test: the migrated validators parse and match expected paths; `check validators` OK; `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
+- [ ] (Behavioral proof — a planted secret producing a confirmed blocker through the engine — lives in the end-to-end task.)

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` for the fixture review test; the rest is content migration. Reuse the existing rule wording; only reformat and re-home. Depends on the focused-validators task so the authoring pattern already exists.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` for the parse/`check validators` tests; the rest is content migration. Reuse the existing rule wording; only reformat, split, and re-home. Depends on the focused-validators task so the authoring pattern already exists.
\ No newline at end of file

-| Validator | Concern | `probes` | severity |
-|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
-| `no-secrets` | secret-looking literals committed to code | (none) | blocker |

-| `test-integrity` | test cheating + the `no-hard-code` "return 42 to pass a test" rule (moved here from code-quality) | (none) | blocker |

-- All are in-file judgments → no probes.

-- [ ] `command-safety` (and a general note) document the real-time→review-time change.
-- [ ] `check validators` reports OK for the migrated set.

+- [ ] Rule bodies/design note state the real-time→review-time change explicitly.
